QF.id: VB Concept and Role of Mutual Fund_30

question.text: Mr. Kumar invests Rs. 10,000 in a mutual fund when the NAV is Rs. 20. How many units will he receive?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Number of units = Investment Amount / NAV = Rs. 10,000 / Rs. 20 = 500 units. Each unit costs Rs. 20, so Rs. 10,000 can buy exactly 500 units. **The Trap:** 200 units would mean each unit costs Rs. 50 (10,000/200). 1,000 units would mean each unit costs Rs. 10. Always divide the investment amount by the NAV to get the number of units. **Cross-Exam Context:** This is also tested in Series V-A (MF Distributors) under unit allotment calculation. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** This is the most basic calculation in mutual funds. Units = Amount / NAV. When clients invest through SIP, they get different numbers of units each month because NAV changes. More units when NAV is low, fewer when NAV is high — this is the benefit of rupee cost averaging.
